reasoning: The unsafe findings are false positives: qt.hook/patch-qt are legitimate package operations, install.sh/updater.sh are not executed during makepkg. PKGBUILD sources are checksum-verified archives.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen overreacted to post-install scripts and misinterpreted patching operations. Deepseek correctly contextualized build/install boundaries.
false_positives: qt.hook: Auto-executing patch-qt is normal for this package; hook is standard
install.sh: Not executed during makepkg; merely installed for user use
updater.sh: Not executed during makepkg; user-triggered operation
patch-qt: Modifies package-owned files in /usr/lib/eID_klient, not system libraries
learnings: Improve prompt: Emphasize that only makepkg-triggered executions (build/package/prepare/check) are in-scope
Train models to recognize AUR-specific patterns: SKIP checksums, archive.org sources, Qt patching workflows
Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install reviews over helper scripts not executed during build
Reduce false alarms on post-install hooks when they call package-owned scripts
